stories.headline: Lazarus Group fingered in $620M April hack wave — Drift and Kelp DAO drove 95%
stories.tldr: April crypto theft hits roughly $620M, with two North Korea-linked exploits accounting for about $577M of the stolen funds.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: DPRK is now harvesting crypto faster than US sanctions can squeeze it, signaling DeFi's human-layer is the new attack surface.
the_details: Drift Protocol lost $285M on April 1 after attackers spent months posing as a trading firm to social-engineer internal teams.
Kelp DAO drained for $292M on April 18, stranding wrapped ether across 20 chains in 2026's worst DeFi exploit.
April is the worst single hack month since Bybit's February 2025 incident, per Phemex and Live Bitcoin News tallies.

stories.headline: Strategy buys 34,164 BTC for $2.54B — biggest haul since 2024, hoard hits 815K
stories.tldr: Michael Saylor's Strategy added 34,164 bitcoin for $2.54 billion, its largest single acquisition since 2024.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: One corporate buyer just absorbed roughly two weeks of net new bitcoin issuance, tightening float as the Fed keeps rates sticky.
the_details: Purchase brings Strategy's stack to 815,061 BTC, putting the position back into modest unrealized profit.
Buy coincided with $1.4B in weekly global crypto fund inflows, led by BTC and ETH products.
BTC briefly tagged $78K on the news plus Trump's Iran ceasefire extension before fading near $77.5K.

stories.headline: CLARITY Act timeline slips into May as Tillis dashes Senate's April hopes
stories.tldr: Senator Thom Tillis told reporters the Senate market-structure compromise will not clear in April, pushing the bill into May.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Every week of slippage shrinks the 2026 legislative window and tilts crypto enforcement back toward an SEC still working from interpretive guidance.
the_details: More than 100 crypto firms including Coinbase and Ripple have signed a letter pressing the Banking Committee to mark up the bill.
Negotiators previously claimed an 'agreement in principle' before April, but the legislative text remains unfinalized.
SEC and CFTC issued a joint 68-page interpretive release in March establishing a five-category token taxonomy.

stories.headline: Bhutan moves 250 BTC; 2026 outflows top $240M as treasury drops 73% from peak
stories.tldr: Bhutan moved another 250 BTC on April 13, pushing 2026 outflows above $240 million from its sovereign mining stash, per Arkham data.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Sovereign sellers like Bhutan and the German Bundeskasse remain a structural overhang against the institutional bid into Strategy and US ETFs.
the_details: Royal Government of Bhutan's holdings have collapsed roughly 73% from their October 2024 peak.
Movements track to centralized exchange deposit addresses, suggesting active distribution rather than internal rebalance.
Selling cadence has accelerated even as Strategy alone bought 34,164 BTC this month.

stories.headline: Vercel breach sends crypto devs scrambling to rotate API keys
stories.tldr: A security incident at hosting provider Vercel forced crypto teams to audit and rotate exposed API keys and deployment secrets across DeFi front ends.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Vercel hosts front ends for many DeFi protocols; leaked keys could enable RPC hijacks, dashboard defacement, or phishing redirects against retail users.
the_details: CoinDesk reported the incident on April 20; affected teams rotated Infura, Alchemy and CoinGecko keys within hours.
Lands amid April's $620M hack tally where attackers increasingly target signing infra and access tokens, not Solidity bugs.
Reinforces the 2026 trend of off-chain platform compromise displacing on-chain smart-contract exploits as the top threat vector.
